mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into higher equity valuations by lowering discount rates and supporting risk-taking, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month when policy rates are declining.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines versus the prior month, or if the sign flips and declining FEDFUNDS is followed by negative median returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above recent trend forces tighter rate expectations and higher discount rates, which depresses equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration months are followed by positive SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the median 20-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional median by at least 50 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE indicates weakening household income and future demand, so broad U.S. equities underperform over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of unemployment-rising episodes, or if negative unemployment shocks fail to outperform the unconditional return distribution over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth and payroll growth both strengthen together, the market reads it as broad real-demand acceleration that supports revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising PAYEMS does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in INDPRO signals an industrial slowdown that leaks into cyclical earnings and credit sentiment, causing negative equity returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its level 2 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after INDPRO declines versus two months earlier in at least 60% of observations, or if the average 5-day return after the trigger is nonnegative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below recent trend signal a tightening in interest-sensitive domestic demand, so equity returns are negative over the next month as macro growth expectations weaken.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdowns are not followed by negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses predictive value once quarterly GDPC1 growth is included over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth and payroll growth both decelerate, the market anticipates a broader earnings slowdown and prices equities lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and PAYEMS does not correspond to negative 60-day SPX returns more often than either indicator alone, or if the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL keeps real rates and discount-rate pressure high, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 2.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined tightening-and-inflation condition is not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if performance is no worse than baseline during tightening cycles across any 2-year rolling window.
